=Introduction=

Ocsigen-i18n provides internationalisation (i18n) support for
OCaml Web applications built with [[site:/eliom|Eliom]].

Translations are described in TSV (tab-separated values) files.
A code generator produces an Eliom file with the translations,
and a PPX extension provides a convenient syntax to use them in
your program.

==Installation

{{{
opam install ocsigen-i18n
}}}

The package provides three executables:
* **ocsigen-i18n-generator**: generates an {{{.eliom}}} file from a TSV file
* **ocsigen-i18n-rewriter**: PPX extension for the {{{[%i18n ...]}}} syntax
* **ocsigen-i18n-checker**: PPX checker for i18n expressions

==Quick start

Create a TSV file (e.g. {{{example_i18n.tsv}}}) with your translations:

{{{
hello	Hello!	Bonjour !
greeting	Hello {{name}}.	Bonjour {{name}}.
}}}

Generate the Eliom file:

{{{
ocsigen-i18n-generator --languages en,fr \
  --input-file example_i18n.tsv \
  --output-file example_i18n.eliom
}}}

Use the PPX in your code:

{{{
[%i18n hello]
[%i18n greeting ~name:[txt "World"]]
}}}

Add the PPX to your dune file:

{{{
(preprocess (pps ocsigen-i18n -- --default-module Example_i18n))
}}}
